channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: World Cup: We’ve suffered injustice, says Egypt coach after loss to Argentina
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Egypt’s coach, Hossam Hassan, claims his team suffered “injustice” in their World Cup defeat to Argentina, citing questionable decisions on and off the pit
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-weve-suffered-injustice-says-egypt-coach-after-loss-to-argentina/

item.title: Tinubu names Gbajabiamila, AGF, others in state police implementation committee
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu forms a committee, led by Gbajabiamila, to draft the National Policing Bill for state police implementation, addressing Nigeria’s security

item.title: Argentina, Messi complete epic comeback vs. Egypt,...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Argentina left it until late again to muscle their way past Egypt in a game that had everything: VAR drama, Messi magic, underdog brilliance and another unforgettable World Cup moment.

item.title: World Cup VAR review: Were Egypt unfairly denied a...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: null

item.title: Messi saves Argentina in dramatic win over Egypt
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Lionel Messi scored in a record sixth straight World Cup knockout match to help Argentina reach the quarterfinals with a stunning late comeback to beat Egypt 3-2 in Atlanta.

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Belgium midfielder Nicolas Raskin said "there's always justice somewhere in life" after beating a U.S. team controversially featuring Folarin Balogun,

item.title: Lautaro told 'emotional' Messi to enjoy 'last WC'
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Lautaro Martinez told a visibly emotional Lionel Messi to enjoy the moment following Argentina's 3-2 triumph over Egypt on Tuesday to qualify for the quarterfinals of the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: U.S. loss sets record as most-watched soccer game
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The United States game against Belgium in the round of 16 at the FIFA World Cup set another record as the most-watched soccer telecast in U.S. history, Fox said after preliminary ratings came out Tuesday.

item.title: Belgium's Onana out of World Cup with ACL injury
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Belgium midfielder Amadou Onana has been ruled out of the World Cup after suffering an anterior cruciate ligament injury, the Red Devils have confirmed.

item.title: 👀 'Overturn this': Belgium troll U.S. after big w...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Belgium defeated the USMNT 4-1 in the 2026 World Cup round-of-16 match Monday night -- and poked fun at the U.S. afterward.

item.title: Galaxy send Pec to Cruzeiro for club-record fee
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The LA Galaxy sent attacker Gabriel Pec to Brazilian side Cruzeiro for a club-record fee, the MLS side announced Tuesday.

item.title: Gotham FC, NYCFC to share new Queens stadium
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Gotham FC will play home games at Etihad Park in Queens, New York, beginning in 2028, putting the reigning NWSL champions inside New York City limits for the first time.

item.title: Egypt bemoan 'injustice' after Argentina defeat
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Hossam Hassan said Egypt were the victim of an "injustice" in their 3-2 World Cup defeat against Argentina due to FIFA "wanting Lionel Messi to stay in the running."

item.title: Tinubu: ‘If you tackle us, we will Asiwaju you’ – Umahi warns Obidient Movement
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Minister of Works, Dave Umahi, has cautioned members of the Obidient Movement trying to undermine President Bola Tinubu’s great works. Umahi vowed to tackle members of the group, stressing that he won’t keep quiet over attempts to undermine Tinubu’s great works. Speaking in Lag

item.title: Ghana Declines Ramaphosa’s State Visit Amid Xenophobic Attacks on Its Citizens in South Africa
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Ghanaian government has reportedly turned down a request for a state visit by South African President Cyril Ramaphosa as concerns mount over recent xenophobic attacks targeting Ghanaians and other foreign nationals in South Africa. According to diplomatic sources in Accra and Pretoria cited by t

item.title: Notorious terrorist commander killed as troops intensify offensive in Katsina, Zamfara​
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force North West, Operation FANSAN YAMMA, have neutralised a notorious terrorist commander, foiled coordinated attacks and recovered weapons during separate military operations in Zamfara and Katsina states. The development was disclosed in a statement issued by the Media In

item.title: Nigeria Mourns Medical Graduate Killed In Russian Attack On Ukraine
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has expressed deep sorrow over the death of Miss Nnani Adaobi Marian, a 23-year-old Nigerian medical graduate of Kharkiv National Medical University, who reportedly died from injuries sustained during a Russian aerial attack on Kharkiv, Ukraine. In a statement issued on Tuesda

item.title: 2026 World Cup: The match was rigged for Argentina – Egypt coach, Hassan
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Egypt head coach. Hossam Hassan, has claimed their last-16 match at the 2026 World Cup was rigged in favour of Argentina. The Pharoahs threw away a two-goal lead to lose 3-2 on Tuesday evening. But some of the officiating calls left Hassan angry after the game. “I will say what’s on my m

item.title: Edo gives businesses ultimatum to install CCTV
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Edo has directed businesses to install CCTV cameras by 30 July to strengthen crime-fighting efforts, but the order has raised questions about its legal basis, affordability, and privacy, drawing contrasting reactions from government and civil society. The post Edo gives businesses ultimatum to insta

item.title: Governors Back Tinubu, Urge Nigerians To Stay Course

item.description: First-term governors elected on the platform of the All Progressives Congress have reaffirmed their support for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu's Renewed Hope Agenda, urging Nigerians to remain patient as the administration's economic reforms take effect. item.title: Viral AI fakes flood social media as Iran mourns Khamenei
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Iran drew huge crowds for former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral procession in Tehran, documented by international news agencies. But alongside genuine images pf mourners, AI-generated videos and photos falsely claiming to show the event have spread widely online. Some exaggerate cro

item.title: Egypt boss slams 'unfair' officiating and claims World Cup 'directed towards Argentina'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan said the referee was "unfair" and claimed the World Cup was "directed towards Argentina" following their dramatic 3-2 defeat in the last 16.

item.title: Dollar Naira Exchange Rate Today, July 7, 2026: Nigerian Naira depreciates across official, black markets
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Naira depreciated on Tuesday against the dollar at the official and parallel foreign exchange markets. The Central Bank of Nigeria’s data showed that the Naira fell to N1,375.75 per dollar on Tuesday, down from N1,368.27 exchanged on Monday. This means that on a day-to-day basis the naira

item.title: Oil prices rise as the U.S. cancels Iran’s license to sell oil
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil futures climbed late Tuesday after the Treasury Department canceled a license granted on June 21 to allow for the sale of Iranian oil.

item.title: 6 Arrested As Mob Kills 2 Travellers In Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kaduna State Police Command has arrested six suspects over the lynching of two travellers who were falsely accused of theft by an angry mob in Birnin Yero, Kaduna State. The victims, identified as Muhammadu Sani, 45, and his friend, Aliyu Mohammed, 40, both from Umar Village in Igabi Local Gover

item.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina’s Messi makes history after missing penalty against Egypt
item.dc:creator: Daily Post

DAILY POST reports that Yasser Ibrahim put Egypt in front in the 15th minute of the encounter. Argentina was then awarded a penalty a few minutes later befo

item.title: PFIPC saga: SERAP condemns alleged arrest of Adeniyi’s father
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has condemned the reported arrest and detention of the father of Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi, who has been parading himself as the Director-General of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC). The post PFIPC

item.title: FEMD Closes Galadimawa Road Over Flooding Risk
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory Emergency Management Department (FEMD) has barricaded the flood-prone slip road at the Galadimawa Interchange in Abuja to prevent the loss of lives and property following forecasts of heavy flooding across the country. The move follows warning by the Nigeria Hydrologica

item.title: ‎’How I Waited For 18 Years Before Having My Twin Children’ -Rykardo Agbor
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nollywood actor Rykardo Agbor has spoken about parenthood, revealing that he waited 18 years to become a parent before finally having twins. While speaking, the actor explained that despite enduring a long period of childlessness, he remained patient and faithful. Rykardo also disclosed that he had

item.dc:creator: PM News

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Tuesday inaugurated the Presidential Working Group on the National Policing Bill to prepare the legal framework for the implementation of state police across the country. Tinubu, represented by his Chief of Staff, Mr. Femi Gbajabiamila, inaugurated the panel at the President

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Several followers joked that Yemi Alade was boxing with emotion after appearing to hug Okocha during the session, while others predicted that Portable would once again have the upper hand when both celebrities meet inside the ring.

item.title: Funke Akindele reveals why she dances to promote her movies
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nollywood filmmaker and actress, Funke Akindele, has explained that her popular dance videos began as a creative solution to promote her films when she could not afford a marketing budget. item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A federal judge rejected an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain information on election workers in Fulton County, Georgia, related to the 2020 vote.

item.title: Cattle Herder Survives Lioness Attack by Staying Calm as Video Goes Viral
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A cattle herder in India has survived a frightening encounter with a lioness after reportedly spending nearly 30 minutes trapped beneath the wild animal in an incident captured on video. The attack happened in Garajiya village in Gujarat’s Bhavnagar district on Sunday, July 5. According to forest of

item.title: EFCC Hands Over Seized Beds, Mattresses To Unity Schools
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Minister of Education, Dr. Tunji Alausa, has said the education sector is the leading beneficiary of the Federal Government’s asset recovery programme, with proceeds of crime now being redirected to improve student welfare and expand access to quality education. Speaking during the handover of r

item.title: Homegrown Catnip Lotion Matches Deet in Repelling Mosquitoes, Uganda Trial Finds
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A locally produced catnip-based lotion has proven to be just as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes during field trials in Uganda, offering a potentially cheaper weapon in the fight against malaria. Researchers from Cardiff University and Uganda presented the findings at the Society for Experi

item.title: Africa's World Cup dream fades
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In tonight's edition, Egypt were seconds away from a historic triumph against Argentina, only to watch their dream vanish in the cruelest fashion. Also, media in Accra claim Ghana has rebuffed Cyril Ramaphosa's request for a state visit over xenophobic attacks. And a single photograph has reignited

item.title: Enugu Dep Governor’s Community Denies Sharing Water With Cows
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Ezimo Community in Udenu Local Government Area of Enugu State, Tuesday, faulted claims by Sahara Reporters that natives of Urukpa settlement of the community share water with grazing cows. THE WHISTLER reports that Ezimo is the country home of the state Deputy Governor, Barr Ifeanyi Ossai. The Presi

item.title: Canadian province sues OpenAI over alleged ChatGPT-linked shooting warnings
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Lawsuit claims OpenAI ignored calls to alert authorities about violent ChatGPT prompts tied to mass shooting suspect.

item.title: APC Primary: Okiemute Urges Diden To Withdraw Suit Against Joel-Onowakpo
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Director-General of the Delta State Traffic Management Authority (DESTMA), Benjamin Essien Okiemute, has appealed to former member of the Delta State House of Assembly, Michael Diden, popularly known as Ejele, to withdraw his court case challenging the emergence of Senator Joel-Onowakpo Thomas a

item.title: Reps to quiz Accountant General over submission of Federation Account for audit
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The House of Representatives on Tuesday expressed grave concern over the non-responsive attitude of the Accountant-General of the Federation (AGF) The post Reps to quiz Accountant General over submission of Federation Account for audit appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Ajaokuta steel secures 20-Year gas supply deal with NNPCL
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: In what is being described as one of the most significant milestones in Nigeria’s industrial history, the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Ajaokuta Steel Company Limited, Prof. Nasir Abdulsalam, has secured a 20-year Gas Supply Agreement and Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the

item.title: COREN Unveils Tougher Sanctions, Revives Tribunal
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) on Tuesday disclosed that it has introduced a tiered sanctions regime and reactivated its disciplinary tribunal. This, the council explained, was part of reforms aimed at tackling engineering misconduct, eliminating quackery and streng

item.title: 'It seems like the politics are starting to drown out some of the excellent football'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Paris' public prosecutor said on Tuesday it had opened an investigation into a Paraguayan lawmaker's comments about France football captain Kylian Mbappe. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Alexander Cooley is Claire Tow Professor of Political Science, Barnard College.

item.title: Trump lashes out at NATO as first day of summit wraps in Turkiye
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Leaders have gathered in Ankara for a two-day summit slated to cover defence spending and the Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Police confirm mob killing of two travellers in Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Kaduna State Police Command has confirmed the killing of two travellers at Gidan Kalu village in Igabi Local Government Area of Kaduna State. The post Police confirm mob killing of two travellers in Kaduna appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Abia Lawmakers Approve Single-Campus Structure For ABSU
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Abia State House of Assembly has passed the Abia State University (Amendment No. 3) Bill, 2026, a legislation aimed at restructuring the Abia State University (ABSU), Uturu, into a single-campus institution. The legislation was passed on Tuesday after scaling its third reading during plenary pre
